about.title: About Me
about.content: I am Shreya Reddy, a Senior AI/ML Engineer with 4+ years building production ML systems across healthcare, fintech, and telecom. I specialize in end-to-end MLOps, real-time feature engineering, LLM-powered RAG applications, and multi-model ensemble deployment on AWS SageMaker and Google Cloud Vertex AI. I have a track record of 30–50% efficiency gains and multi-million-dollar revenue impact through scalable ML infrastructure, GenAI agents, and regulatory-compliant model governance.

skills.title: Skills & Expertise
skills.content: I have a diverse skill set spanning ML/AI, data engineering, cloud platforms, MLOps, and full-stack development.

education.title: Education
education.content: I hold a Master of Science in Data Analytics Engineering from George Mason University, Fairfax, VA (Aug 2023 – May 2025) and a Bachelor of Technology in Information Technology from Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Hyderabad, India (Aug 2019 – Jun 2023).

fun.title: Fun & Hobbies
fun.content: In my free time, I love going for walks and runs — it's my way of clearing my head and staying energized. I'm also a big café explorer! Discovering new coffee spots and trying different brews is something I genuinely look forward to. Doodling is my favorite hobby — I can lose track of time just sketching random things. And when I'm at home, I love organizing and styling my space to make it feel just right, because a well-arranged space genuinely lifts my mood and helps me feel my best.

experience.title: Professional Experience
experience.content: I have experience building production ML systems across healthcare, fintech, and telecom, contributing to both AI/ML engineering and data engineering roles.

highlights: Migrated pharmaceutical supply chain ML pipelines from Databricks to Google Cloud Vertex AI, rebuilding drug shortage risk scoring using XGBoost and LSTM ensemble for scalable production deployment

Designed real-time feature pipelines on Pub/Sub and Kafka ingesting EHR events, supplier fill-rate streams, and external signals into BigQuery, replacing overnight batch processing with sub 3-minute feature latency
Built an internal RAG-based procurement assistant using LangChain and Pinecone, enabling supply chain teams to query drug shortage policies and inventory reports in natural language

Deployed risk score endpoints via Cloud Run through an Apigee-secured REST API with automated retraining on Vertex AI Pipelines, consumed by InteLogix dashboards and SAP ERP systems for auto reorder triggers
Developed a three-model credit risk scoring system on AWS SageMaker for card application decisioning, limit optimization, and collections propensity using XGBoost, LightGBM, and Gradient Boosting
Engineered a centralized feature store on SageMaker Feature Store with signals including payment ratio, utilization trend, and delinquency history across batch and real-time scoring workloads
Integrated SHAP explainability via SageMaker Clarify to generate adverse action reasons for declined applications, meeting ECOA and Regulation B compliance requirements
Maintained automated retraining and drift monitoring using SageMaker Pipelines and MLflow across weekly production deployments
Built batch ingestion pipelines using AWS Glue and PySpark to migrate telecom customer usage and billing data from on-prem Oracle systems into Amazon S3
Designed Redshift data warehouse tables and optimized SQL transformation scripts to support incremental loads, enabling business intelligence teams to run churn and revenue analytics
Automated daily pipeline scheduling using Apache Airflow across S3, Glue, and Redshift, handling failure alerts and retry logic to maintain data freshness for client reporting SLAs

projects.title: Projects
projects.content: My projects span AI-powered portfolio applications, production ML systems in healthcare and fintech, and large-scale data engineering pipelines in telecom.

items.name: AI Portfolio — This Website
highlights: Built a fully interactive AI-powered personal portfolio where an LLM answers questions about my background, skills, and experience in real time
Implemented a RAG pipeline using Mistral embeddings and vector similarity search over a structured knowledge base to ground AI responses in accurate personal data
Designed a multi-section chat UI with section-specific AI responses for About, Experience, Projects, Skills, Education, and Contact
Engineered a Node.js/Express backend with an ingest pipeline that indexes knowledge into an in-memory vector store for semantic retrieval
Built with React, TailwindCSS, and Framer Motion for smooth animated UI including expandable project cards and a fluid canvas background

items.name: Pharmaceutical Supply Chain ML System — Cardinal Health
highlights: Migrated pharmaceutical supply chain ML pipelines from Databricks to Google Cloud Vertex AI, rebuilding drug shortage risk scoring using an XGBoost and LSTM ensemble
Designed real-time feature pipelines on Pub/Sub and Kafka ingesting EHR events, supplier fill-rate streams, and external signals into BigQuery with sub 3-minute feature latency
Built an internal RAG-based procurement assistant using LangChain and Pinecone, enabling supply chain teams to query drug shortage policies and inventory reports in natural language
Deployed risk score endpoints via Cloud Run through an Apigee-secured REST API with automated retraining on Vertex AI Pipelines, consumed by InteLogix dashboards and SAP ERP systems

items.name: Credit Risk Scoring System — Discover Financial Services
highlights: Developed a three-model credit risk scoring system on AWS SageMaker for card application decisioning, limit optimization, and collections propensity
Engineered a centralized feature store on SageMaker Feature Store with payment ratio, utilization trend, and delinquency history signals across batch and real-time workloads
Integrated SHAP explainability via SageMaker Clarify to generate adverse action reasons for declined applications, meeting ECOA and Regulation B compliance requirements
Maintained automated retraining and drift monitoring using SageMaker Pipelines and MLflow across weekly production deployments

items.name: Telecom Data Engineering Pipeline — Tech Mahindra
highlights: Built batch ingestion pipelines using AWS Glue and PySpark to migrate telecom customer usage and billing data from on-prem Oracle systems into Amazon S3
Designed Redshift data warehouse tables and optimized SQL transformation scripts to support incremental loads for churn and revenue analytics
Automated daily pipeline scheduling using Apache Airflow across S3, Glue, and Redshift with failure alerts and retry logic to maintain data freshness for client SLAs

availability.title: Job Availability & Career
availability.content: I am currently open to new opportunities in AI/ML Engineering, MLOps, and Data Engineering. I am actively looking for full-time roles where I can contribute to production ML systems, GenAI applications, and scalable data infrastructure. My ideal role involves LLM-powered pipelines, cloud ML platforms like AWS SageMaker or Google Cloud Vertex AI, and real-world impact across industries like healthcare, fintech, or tech. I completed my Master of Science in Data Analytics Engineering from George Mason University in May 2025 and am based in the US with full work authorization. I can start immediately or within a short notice period depending on the role. My salary expectation is $120,000 per year.
availability.careerGap: There is no significant experience gap in my career. After completing my role at Tech Mahindra in July 2023, I transitioned directly into my Master's program at George Mason University starting August 2023. During graduate school, I also gained industry experience — I worked as a Machine Learning Engineer at Discover Financial Services in May–June 2024 and joined Cardinal Health as an AI/ML Engineer in June 2024. So my graduate studies and professional work overlapped, and I have been continuously building production ML skills throughout.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: You can reach me directly via email or phone, and explore my work on LinkedIn and GitHub.
